headline: Context management and agent skills lead a broad rotation toward technique-level work on how AI agents process information.
body: Context management tops the movers while agent skills, vulnerability detection, behavioral auditing, ablation, masking, and Gradient Staleness all break out together, signaling a shift toward the mechanics of feeding and auditing agents rather than model releases alone. Practitioner material reinforces this, with new walkthroughs on correcting coding-agent context setup and codebase harnesses [3] and explainers on Model Context Protocols as the channel for external agent access [0][2]. On the model side, Meituan released LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion parameter Mixture-of-Experts model activating roughly 48 billion parameters per token and trained on AI ASIC superpods rather than Nvidia GPUs [5]. OpenAI's Greg Brockman framed the next step as merging chat and agents into persistent agents, with compute as the scarce resource [1].
